additionalTime: 1 hour dough chill, 30 minutes shaped-crust chill, and at least 4 hours cooling
author: Robert Carr
description: A deep-dish blueberry pie with a vivid fresh-and-wild blueberry lemon filling, a flaky egg-white-sealed butter crust, and a crisp nut-free brown butter lemon-oat crumble.

groceryIngredients.note: use the white to seal the crust
for 5 teaspoons zest and 3 tablespoons juice

ingredients: 1 2/3 cups (200 grams) all-purpose flour, for the crust
1 tablespoon (12 grams) granulated sugar, for the crust
1 teaspoon (3 grams) Diamond Crystal kosher salt, for the crust
10 tablespoons (142 grams) cold unsalted butter, cut into 1/2-inch cubes, for the crust
1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar
4 to 5 tablespoons ice water
2 tablespoons all-purpose flour, for rolling the crust
1 large egg white, lightly beaten, for sealing the crust
24 ounces (680 grams) fresh cultivated blueberries, about 4 cups
8 ounces (225 grams) frozen wild blueberries, about 2 cups, kept frozen
5 teaspoons finely grated lemon zest, divided, from about 3 large lemons
3 tablespoons (45 grams) fresh lemon juice
1 cup plus 2 tablespoons (225 grams) granulated sugar, for the filling
45 grams cassava-based tapioca starch
1/2 teaspoon (2 grams) Diamond Crystal kosher salt, for the filling
1/4 teaspoon ground coriander
8 tablespoons (113 grams) unsalted butter, for the crumble
1 tablespoon room-temperature water, for the crumble
1 cup (120 grams) all-purpose flour, for the crumble
1/2 cup (45 grams) old-fashioned rolled oats
1/2 cup packed (106 grams) light brown sugar
1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt, for the crumble
instructions: Make the crust dough: in a medium bowl, whisk together 1 2/3 cups (200 grams) all-purpose flour, 1 tablespoon (12 grams) granulated sugar, and 1 teaspoon (3 grams) Diamond Crystal kosher salt.

Add 10 tablespoons (142 grams) cold cubed unsalted butter. Cut the butter into the flour with a pastry cutter or fork until most pieces are pea-size and several remain about the size of small almonds.

Combine 1 teaspoon apple cider vinegar with 4 tablespoons ice water. Drizzle it over the flour-butter mixture while tossing with a fork. Squeeze a handful of dough; if it holds together without crumbling, stop. If dry flour remains, add the final 1 tablespoon ice water 1 teaspoon at a time.

Turn the shaggy dough onto a clean surface and press it into a rough rectangle. Fold it into thirds like a letter, then gently press it into a 1-inch-thick disk. Wrap tightly and refrigerate for at least 1 hour.

Make the crumble while the dough chills: melt 8 tablespoons (113 grams) unsalted butter in a light-colored medium saucepan over medium heat. Cook, stirring and scraping the bottom often, until the sputtering subsides and the milk solids turn deep golden brown, about 5 to 7 minutes after the butter melts.

Immediately pour the brown butter, including all browned bits, into a medium heatproof bowl. Let it cool for 10 minutes, then slowly stir in 1 tablespoon room-temperature water to replace the moisture lost during browning.

Combine 1/2 cup packed (106 grams) light brown sugar and 2 teaspoons of the lemon zest in a small bowl. Rub the zest into the sugar with your fingertips until the sugar feels slightly damp and smells strongly of lemon.

Stir the lemon-infused brown sugar, 1 cup (120 grams) all-purpose flour, 1/2 cup (45 grams) old-fashioned rolled oats, and 1/2 teaspoon Diamond Crystal kosher salt into the brown butter until no dry flour remains and moist crumbs form. Squeeze some of the mixture into irregular 1/2-inch clumps, cover, and refrigerate.
Remove the chilled crust dough from the refrigerator. If it is too firm to roll without cracking, let it stand at room temperature for 5 to 10 minutes.

Dust the work surface and rolling pin with up to 2 tablespoons all-purpose flour. Roll the dough from the center outward into a 14-inch round, rotating it frequently and dusting underneath only as needed to prevent sticking.

Gently settle the dough into a 9-inch deep-dish metal pie pan without stretching it. Press it firmly against the bottom and sides, trim the overhang to 1 inch, tuck the overhang under itself, and crimp the edge. Refrigerate the shaped crust for 30 minutes.

While the crust chills, place a baking steel or pizza stone on the lower-middle oven rack with a rimmed baking sheet on top. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F for at least 30 minutes. If no steel or stone is available, preheat the rimmed baking sheet by itself.

Line the chilled crust with parchment paper and fill it to the top with pie weights or dried beans, pressing the weights gently against the sides. Place the pie pan on the hot rimmed baking sheet and bake for 20 minutes.

Carefully remove the parchment and pie weights. Brush the bottom and sides of the hot crust with the lightly beaten egg white, using a thin coat without puddles. Return the crust to the hot baking sheet and bake for 5 to 7 minutes, until the surface looks dry and the bottom is just beginning to color. Let the crust cool for 10 minutes.

Make the filling while the crust cools: combine 1 cup plus 2 tablespoons (225 grams) granulated sugar and the remaining 1 tablespoon lemon zest in a large mixing bowl. Rub the zest into the sugar with your fingertips until the sugar feels damp and highly fragrant.
Whisk 45 grams cassava-based tapioca starch, 1/2 teaspoon (2 grams) Diamond Crystal kosher salt, and 1/4 teaspoon ground coriander into the lemon sugar until evenly distributed with no starch clumps.

Add 24 ounces (680 grams) fresh cultivated blueberries, 8 ounces (225 grams) frozen wild blueberries, and 3 tablespoons (45 grams) fresh lemon juice. Fold gently with a flexible spatula until every berry is evenly coated. Let the filling stand for 15 minutes, then fold it once more from the bottom to redistribute the juices and starch.
Scrape the filling and every drop of accumulated juice into the par-baked crust. Spread the berries evenly without crushing them.
Scatter the chilled crumble evenly over the filling, keeping the pieces irregular and leaving several small gaps where the blueberry filling remains visible so steam can escape.
Bake the assembled pie on the hot rimmed baking sheet at 400 degrees F for 20 minutes.

Reduce the oven temperature to 375 degrees F and continue baking for 45 to 60 minutes. The pie is done when the crumble is deeply golden and the filling bubbles slowly and thickly in several places near the center, not only around the edge. The center of the filling should register about 210 to 213 degrees F, depending on elevation.

Shield the crimped crust edge with foil if it becomes deeply browned. If the crumble reaches a deep golden brown before the filling bubbles at the center, loosely tent the entire pie with foil and continue baking.
Transfer the pie to a wire rack and cool completely, uncovered, for at least 4 hours and preferably until the center is no warmer than 85 degrees F. Do not slice it while warm.
notes: This recipe is formulated by weight because the sugar and tapioca starch ratios depend on the weight of the fruit. Use the gram measurements whenever possible.
Use cassava-based tapioca starch rather than quick-cooking tapioca pearls. Do not substitute the same quantity of cornstarch, flour, or Instant ClearJel because each thickener behaves differently.
Do not thaw the frozen wild blueberries. They provide a deeper, more concentrated blueberry flavor while the fresh cultivated berries provide larger, juicier fruit.
The filling is intentionally not precooked. Baking the raw berries preserves their natural juice and fresher blueberry flavor instead of turning part of the filling into a cooked jam.
Do not reduce the filling sugar. Its quantity is balanced with the fruit and tapioca starch so the starch sets correctly without becoming pasty.
The coriander should not be identifiable as a spice. At 1/4 teaspoon, it reinforces the floral and citrus-like aromas already present in blueberries.
The nut-free crumble contains no cinnamon, vanilla, or almond extract so the dominant flavors remain blueberry, lemon, brown butter, and toasted oats.
Adding 1 tablespoon water to the brown butter replaces moisture lost during browning and keeps the crumble crisp and clumpy rather than greasy or sandy.
The egg white coating and partial blind bake protect the bottom crust from the raw berry filling.
The pie is not done when only the outer filling bubbles. Wait for slow, thick bubbles near the center so the tapioca starch fully activates.
Cooling to 85 degrees F or below is essential for clean slices. The filling will be loose if the pie is cut while warm.
For the crispest crust and crumble, serve the pie the day it is baked. Store leftovers loosely covered at room temperature for up to 2 days or refrigerated for up to 4 days.
To refresh refrigerated slices, warm them on a baking sheet in a 350 degrees F oven for 8 to 10 minutes, then let them stand for 10 minutes before serving.
title: Blueberry Lemon Crumble Pie
